message:
fix: 3 index test failures from WAL dedup refactor

search: Add NaN/Inf score filtering in scan_wal and scan_wal_collect_all.
The ANN path (two_tier.rs) already filtered non-finite WAL scores, but
the brute-force path did not, causing NaN-scored entries to pollute top-k.

lib: Update append_duplicate_doc_id_both_searchable test to match the
WAL-shadows-main contract introduced in a3f50775. When a WAL entry
exists for a doc_id, the main-index entry is intentionally suppressed.

repro_soft_delete_rollback: Verify rollback via search (which includes
WAL) instead of main-index flag inspection. The append() call now
tombstones main-index entries as a best-effort freshness optimization,
so the main-index entry is already tombstoned before soft_delete runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
